title: Diporidium macranthum
taxonomy: Diporidium macranthum is a genus of extinct plant that belongs to the family of Bennettitales, a group of seed ferns. This ancient plant was part of the group known as Cycadeoidae, characterized by the presence of cycad-like features. The classification of Diporidium macranthum places it within the broader category of extinct seed plants from the Mesozoic Era. The genus is often associated with other Bennettitales, which were prevalent during the time of the dinosaurs. Its classification as a Bennettitalean plant provides valuable insights into the evolution of seed plants during that period.

intro: Diporidium macranthum, also known as Macranthium, is a genus of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Mexico and Central America, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Diporidium macranthum include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to promote digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems.
